method: HOUSECARL_SKILL_AUTHORING §6.5 fresh-context agent fan-out: one fresh agent per query (20 total), each given ONLY the skill's description verbatim as an anonymized capability statement (no skill name) plus its single query, asked a pure relevance question with reasoning + YES/NO verdict. Judges: Opus, high reasoning effort, no tools. Verdicts adjudicated from the reasoning paragraphs per §6.5 step 4.
description_measured: the shipped frontmatter description (911 chars, 145 words), unmodified

per_query.basis: modded healing potion too strong -> ALCH rebalance, core purpose
mod food -> Requiem nutrition = effects on the ALCH record, not a separate runtime job
custom mead effects+value = alcohol derivation, named in description
skooma price+drawbacks = ALCH value + effects, skooma named verbatim
ingredient four-effect setup = INGR quartet, named verbatim
whole-plugin sweets pass = per-consumable balance pass
stew craftable at cookpot = COBJ recipe, named trigger
weak poisons = magnitude+price ladders, core ALCH fields
instant-heal cheese wheel = the vanilla-food anti-pattern this skill replaces
wine keywords + inebriation = alcohol kit derivation
greatsword damage = WEAP domain -> weapon skill
brand-new shout-drain effect = MGEF design -> magic skill
loot/vendor appearance = leveled-list/placement, not record balance
SkyPatcher ini = runtime-ini patching, not ESP override derivation
KID keyword distribution = kid-authoring, no consumable balanced
best-potions loadout = gameplay advice, not record authoring
NPC perks/factions = NPC_ domain -> npc skill
soup eating animation clipping = OAR config, not a data-layer edit
drinking fountain = ACTI/world placement, not an inventory ingestible
scripted potion's broken script property = VMAD/script layer -> script skill

notes: Both §6.3 gates passed with margin. All 20 reasoning paragraphs judged on the record-domain/task-kind basis rather than keyword overlap; no meta-confusion observed (description contained no skill name). Judges ran per Aaron's standing model rule: Opus, high effort.
